top_findings.title: Server panics on startup: duplicate route registrations
top_findings.evidence: main.go:295-299 registers /books twice (handleGetBooks, handleCreateBook) and /books/ three times (handleGetBook, handleUpdateBook, handleDeleteBook) on http.DefaultServeMux; running the binary crashes with 'panic: http: multiple registrations for /books' at main.go:297 (confirmed by executing ./bookapi).
top_findings.suggestion: Register one handler per pattern that dispatches on r.Method internally (handlers already guard on method): http.HandleFunc("/books", store.handleBooks) and http.HandleFunc("/books/", store.handleBookByID).

top_findings.title: POST /books unreachable as an HTTP endpoint
top_findings.evidence: handleCreateBook (main.go:187) is correct and unit-tested, but its route registration (main.go:297) both panics at startup and is shadowed by handleGetBooks registered first on /books (main.go:295) — even without the panic, ServeMux keeps only the first handler per pattern, so POST would never reach the create handler.
top_findings.suggestion: Dispatch POST inside a single /books handler based on r.Method.

top_findings.title: PUT /books/{id} unreachable and never returns 404 for a missing id
top_findings.evidence: handleUpdateBook (main.go:218) is shadowed on /books/ (main.go:296 registered first) and unreachable at runtime; additionally UpdateBook uses db.Exec (main.go:107) which never returns sql.ErrNoRows, so the err==sql.ErrNoRows 404 branch (main.go:248) is dead — updating a non-existent id returns 200.
top_findings.suggestion: Dispatch PUT inside one /books/ handler; check RowsAffected()==0 to return 404.

top_findings.title: DELETE /books/{id} unreachable and never returns 404 for a missing id
top_findings.evidence: handleDeleteBook (main.go:259) is shadowed on /books/ (main.go:296) and unreachable at runtime; DeleteBook uses db.Exec (main.go:117) which never yields sql.ErrNoRows, so the 404 branch (main.go:273) is dead — deleting a non-existent id returns 200.
top_findings.suggestion: Dispatch DELETE inside one /books/ handler; check RowsAffected()==0 to return 404.
